items.summary: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

items.title: Disarming Hezbollah in Lebanon is unlikely to be as easy as Donald Trump suggests
items.summary: Donald Trump sold a ceasefire in Lebanon as a step towards peace. But that promise hinges on a condition that Lebanon may not be able to deliver.

items.title: Australia banks on Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark in drone warfare shift

items.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains tax changes

items.summary: The Australian Securities Exchange is within striking distance of new records as economists issue worst-case warnings of a close call for a global recession.

items.summary: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

items.summary: The dark comedy series returns with a biting look at generational conflict, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac as a "combustible" middle-aged couple.

items.title: Iran war and energy shock make delivering the federal budget unusually hard

items.title: As Iran war fallout spreads, the focus shifts to Trump's mind — as it should
items.summary: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

items.title: Refinery blaze will not bump Australia to stage three of national fuel response

items.summary: Police allege two boys armed with machetes set fire to a bar in Southbank early today.

items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds
items.summary: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

items.title: Coca-Cola suggests it could abandon Queensland Containers for Change scheme amid proposed reforms

items.title: Myanmar's coup leader seeks legitimacy as family wealth surfaces in Thailand

items.title: Greyhound industry phase-out a sticking point for Liberals
items.summary: The ban on greyhound racing in Tasmania was part of a deal made to keep the Rockliff Liberals in power. But getting that ban past parliament is proving to be a mongrel of a job. Meanwhile, Labor seems in denial over the issue.

items.title: Family of 13 becomes homeless amid rental shortage, highlighting national crisis
items.summary: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.
